commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Release WebGate FW3.7

- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

commit.message:
fix: cross-process broadcast and result delivery for multi-worker uvicorn

The backend runs 4 uvicorn workers (separate processes). Two systems
relied on in-memory state that doesn't work across processes:

1. Training result delivery: worker callback wrote to in-memory dict/event,
   but the build loop waiting on the event was in a different process.
   Builds would get stuck at 0/N iterations forever.

2. WebSocket broadcast: build loop broadcast messages to in-memory WS
   connections, but the user's WebSocket was connected to a different
   process. UI never received progress updates.

Fixes:
- Result delivery now uses a DB table (worker_job_results) — the callback
  writes results to PG, the build loop polls every 10s.
- Broadcast now uses PG LISTEN/NOTIFY — each worker opens a persistent
  asyncpg connection. When any process broadcasts, NOTIFY relays the
  message to all other processes which deliver to their local WS clients.
- Messages >7.5KB (PG NOTIFY limit) are delivered locally only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(P4.1): Knowledge Graph — concept/method extraction + DB models

- Add GraphNode + GraphEdge SQLAlchemy models (graph_nodes/graph_edges tables)
- Add concepts/methods/related_themes fields to DEEP_PASS3_PROMPT schema
  (max 5 each, only from extracted text — anti-hallucination enforced)

  paper→concept (uses_concept), paper→method (uses_method), paper→author
  (authored_by) edges after each deep analysis

commit.message:
fix: stop WebSocket resource updates causing yo-yo oscillation

WebSocket onResourcesUpdate was pushing metal/crystal/deuterium amounts
into planet state. The backend WS sends real-time computed values (higher
than DB), then fetchPlanet returns the lower DB-persisted value.
In useRealtimeResources, prevServerRef captured the high WS value, then
resolveBase saw the lower DB value as a "deduction" and jumped down.
This cycle (WS jump up → fetchPlanet jump down) caused the visible yo-yo.

Fix: only update energy_ratio from WS (not extrapolated by the hook).
Metal/crystal/deuterium are handled entirely by useRealtimeResources
extrapolation between fetchPlanet calls — no WS interference needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add LLM discovery signals — robots.txt, HTTP headers, HTML meta/comments

Tell AI crawlers where to find readable content (since this is a JS SPA):

1. robots.txt: Llms-txt + Llms-full-txt directives (read first by all crawlers)
2. vercel.json: HTTP Link header + X-Llms-Txt header on all HTML responses
3. index.html <head>: <link rel="alternate" type="text/plain"> to llms-full.txt
4. index.html <head>: <meta name="ai-content-source"> pointing to llms-full.txt
5. index.html <body>: HTML comment directing AI crawlers to llms-full.txt

Five layers of discovery — crawlers that can't execute JS now have
clear, machine-readable pointers to the full plain-text content.

commit.message:
Add banner analytics funnel, conversion tracking, and S2S postbacks

Full funnel: impression → hover → click → landing → conversion with
source attribution (src, click_id) through the entire chain. S2S
postback fires on social_click/content_click to configured ad networks.

Backend: hover endpoint (1x1 pixel), ad_sources CRUD, funnel analytics
by source, postback retry cron. Frontend: click_id/src propagation via
sessionStorage, content_click tracking, Promo admin with Statistics and
Settings tabs. Migrations for ad_sources, conversion_postbacks (PG) and
updated materialized views (CH).

commit.message:
Fix GZip Content-Length mismatch and thread-safety in vector bg job

Remove explicit Content-Length from StreamingResponse to prevent mismatch
when GZipMiddleware compresses the ~50MB response (client would hang waiting
for declared bytes that never arrive). Use chunked transfer encoding instead.

Copy phase_timings dict on first _bg_jobs update to prevent race condition
where status endpoint could read a partially-mutated reference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(proxy_daemon): Map Local 응답에 Content-Length 헤더 추가

크기를 알 수 없어 렌더링이 불완전할 수 있음.
